name: Microplan
title: WHO SMART Microplan Implementation Guide (DRAFT)
description: FHIR Implementation Guide for immunization campaign microplanning, supporting campaign definition, task generation, and execution tracking.

resource.name: Campaign Location
resource.description: Links a resource to a location in the campaign hierarchy.

resource.description: Post-campaign rapid coverage monitoring at Riverside Health Center catchment.

resource.description: Example province-level location.

resource.description: Types of activities performed during an immunization campaign.

resource.description: Types of activities performed during an immunization campaign.

resource.name: Microplan CarePlan
resource.description: An instantiated immunization campaign for a specific geographic area and time period.

resource.name: Microplan Location
resource.description: A location in the immunization campaign hierarchy, from country down to vaccination site.

resource.name: Microplan Location Type CodeSystem
resource.description: Administrative levels in the immunization campaign location hierarchy.

resource.description: Administrative levels in the immunization campaign location hierarchy.

resource.name: Microplan Participant Role CodeSystem
resource.description: Roles of participants in immunization campaign activities.

resource.description: Roles of participants in immunization campaign activities.

resource.name: Microplan PlanDefinition
resource.description: A campaign template that defines activities and which location hierarchy levels they target.

resource.name: Microplan Target Population
resource.description: Estimated target population for vaccination at a specific location.

resource.name: Microplan Task
resource.description: A concrete campaign activity assigned to a team at a specific location and time.

resource.name: Polio SIA Campaign
resource.description: Campaign template for a Polio Supplementary Immunization Activity targeting children 0–59 months.

resource.description: Instantiated Polio SIA campaign for Riverside District.

resource.description: Administer bivalent oral polio vaccine (bOPV) to all children 0–59 months at a vaccination site.

resource.description: Post-campaign rapid coverage assessment at health facility catchment area level.
